evidence: we have invested more than $200 billion in research and development in the last five years in support of these efforts
building AI-optimized infrastructure, including our custom TPUs, and integrating AI capabilities into new and existing products and services
To meet the compute capacity demands of AI training and inference...we are entering into significant leasing arrangements with third party operators
Today, all 15 of our half-billion-user products including seven with two billion users use our Gemini models
AI Overviews makes it easier to ask Google anything...AI Mode allows users to ask more nuanced questions...using Gemini's advanced reasoning, thinking, and multimodal capabilities
Waymo is now providing fully autonomous, paid ride-hailing services to customers in multiple cities
our implementation of AI systems could subject us to competitive harm, regulatory action, legal liability...and brand or reputational harm

AI's energy and water demands have made efforts to reduce our emissions more complex...energy supply is constrained globally due to the significant increase in demand for...AI compute

capex_mentions.quote: invested more than $200 billion in research and development in the last five years
capex_mentions.context: Alphabet total R&D over five years supporting AI and other products; not purely AI-attributable but AI is a stated primary focus

capex_mentions.quote: entering into significant leasing arrangements with third party operators
capex_mentions.context: AI training and inference compute capacity demands driving large-scale infrastructure leasing

capex_mentions.quote: industry supply capacity for AI accelerators, including GPUs as well as our custom-built TPUs, is highly competitive and rapidly evolving
capex_mentions.context: AI accelerator procurement described as a significant and contested cost item

limitations: The $200B R&D figure covers all R&D over five years, not exclusively AI-attributable spend; no single-year AI-specific dollar figure is disclosed in the excerpts, slightly limiting capex precision.

ai_narrative_concreteness scored 5 based on deployment evidence (Gemini in 15 products, Waymo paid rides) but quantified business-outcome metrics (revenue lift, cost savings %) are not present in excerpts; score reflects strong deployment evidence with partial quantification.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth scored 4 (company-specific risk scenarios with mechanisms) rather than 5 because no named AI risk oversight body, formal quantification of AI risk exposure, or structured mitigation program is disclosed in the excerpts.
